outcome_text: Mechanisms are in place to inventory AI systems and are resourced according to organizational risk priorities.
scoring_text: Skill in designing and maintaining AI system inventory mechanisms, including cataloging deployed AI systems, tracking system attributes relevant to risk assessment, and allocating inventory resources according to organizational risk priorities.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in designing and maintaining AI system inventory mechanisms, including cataloging deployed AI systems, tracking system attributes relevant to risk assessment, and allocating inventory resources according to organizational risk priorities.
reference_document_element_description: Establish and maintain the enterprise AI inventory, cataloging all AI systems in production with risk tier, owner, training data provenance, model version, and review status.
comments: NIST AI RMF GV-1.6 (GOVERN) equal WIDAI AG domain. AG-T-002 is the strongest match (STS: 0.753, cosine: 0.748). Secondary: AG-T-005 (AG, cosine: 0.737), AG-K-002 (AG, cosine: 0.710).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the risk management outcome) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 7.5/10 (raw 0.753). Shared vocabulary: [ai, cataloging, inventory, risk, systems]. NIST AI RMF competency implication terms [according, allocating, assessment, attributes, deployed, designing] vs WIDAI terms [data, enterprise, establish, maintain, model, owner] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.896 (P=0.901, R=0.890). Balanced P/R — the 30-word competency implication and 25-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (98.7%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the NIST AI RMF element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.748 ≈ STS 0.753 (gap 0.005). Independent encoding and cross-attention agree. Shared terms [ai, cataloging, inventory, risk, systems] are sufficient for independent encoding to detect the match.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 17.5%. Shared tokens: [ai, cataloging, inventory, risk, systems]. NIST AI RMF-only: [according, allocating, assessment, attributes, deployed, designing]. WIDAI-only: [data, enterprise, establish, maintain, model, owner].
